sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Milan are in excellent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 8 matches, with 4 wins in their recent 5 games, including a convincing 3-0 away victory against Bologna. Pisa, on the other hand, are struggling, without a win in their last 13 matches, managing only 3 draws in their last 5 outings, with heavy losses like the 6-2 defeat to Inter highlighting defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Milan have the upper hand, remaining unbeaten against Pisa with a 2-2 draw in their most recent clash in October 2025 and a 1-0 win back in 1991. Pisa have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 meetings with Milan, often conceding late goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Milan sit 2nd in the league with 50 points, boasting a strong attack (38 goals scored) and the second-best defense (17 conceded). Pisa, languishing in 19th with just 15 points, have scored only 19 goals while conceding 40, ranking among the worst defensively in the league.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Milan’s Adrien Rabiot has been in fine form, averaging a 7.92 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches with 3 goals. Pisa will rely on Stefano Moreo, who has contributed 5 goals this season, to challenge Milan’s solid backline led by Strahinja Pavlović.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Milan’s ability to score across different periods, particularly between 46-60 minutes (17.6% of goals), could exploit Pisa’s tendency to concede late in halves. Pisa’s strength in aerial duels (54.1% won, 3rd in league) may offer some resistance, but their low possession (40.6%) and poor conversion rate (24.4%) suggest they’ll struggle to create chances.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Milan’s superior form, league standing, and historical edge, they are heavily favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 71% probability for an away victory. Pisa’s defensive vulnerabilities and lack of wins make an upset unlikely, though they might nick a goal given recent scoring against Milan.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Milan leads Pisa by a narrow margin of 1-0, thanks to a well-taken header by Ruben Loftus-Cheek in the 39th minute. The first half saw Milan dominate possession and create more consistent pressure, while Pisa struggled to convert their limited chances. Both teams showed moments of intent, but Milan’s superior duel-winning ability and composure in key areas gave them the edge. Pisa will need to regroup and find a way to penetrate Milan’s defense if they hope to level the score in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Milan showcased their superiority in ball control and physicality in the first half, winning a higher percentage of duels and aerial battles. Their ability to maintain possession allowed them to dictate the tempo of the game, and they capitalized on a crucial opportunity late in the half to take the lead. Pisa, on the other hand, showed resilience in defense but lacked the cutting edge in the final third, missing a big chance that could have changed the narrative. Despite having fewer entries into dangerous areas compared to Milan, Pisa’s determination to fight for possession in key zones hints at potential for a comeback if they can be more clinical.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Milan, Ruben Loftus-Cheek was the standout performer, not only for his goal but for his constant threat in advanced positions. His presence in the box proved decisive, and he’ll likely remain a focal point in the second half. On Pisa’s side, players like Stefano Moreo showed creativity with a big chance created, while Filip Stojilković came close to scoring but couldn’t convert. Pisa will need these players to step up if they’re to trouble Milan’s defense after the break. Milan’s defensive line, anchored by Strahinja Pavlović and Matteo Gabbia, has been solid, limiting Pisa’s opportunities effectively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum insights
halftimeSections.text: The first half started with Milan showing early intent, maintaining consistent attacking pressure for a prolonged period. Pisa struggled to respond initially but gradually found their footing with a notable spell of momentum in the middle of the half, pushing Milan back temporarily. However, Milan regained control with a strong surge leading up to their goal, underlining their ability to shift gears when needed. Towards the end of the half, Milan continued to press, while Pisa’s late push showed signs of life but lacked the final product. This ebb and flow suggests both teams can create dangerous moments, but Milan’s attacking spells have been more decisive so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Milan is likely to continue leveraging their possession and duel-winning strengths to control the game and seek a second goal to secure the result. Pisa, trailing by one, will need to take more risks, potentially leaving gaps at the back that Milan could exploit. If Pisa can capitalize on their final third entries and convert a chance early, they could make it a contest. However, Milan’s defensive organization and attacking threat make them the favorites to extend their lead. Expect an open game as Pisa pushes for an equalizer, but Milan’s quality should shine through.
